title: Who won the 2025-26 Ligue 1 (French top-flight football)?
rules: If PSG wins the Ligue 1, then the market resolves to Yes.
tavily_brief: [redacted] Betting odds from August 2025 placed PSG at 1/10 to 1/14 across major sportsbooks, while the Opta supercomputer assigned them a 78.7% title probability after simulating 10,000 season scenarios (per Khelnow and All Football). This reflects PSG's recent dominance: they won four consecutive Ligue 1 titles entering 2025-26 and completed an unprecedented quadruple in 2024-25 (league, two domestic cups, and Champions League), finishing 19 points clear of second-place Marseille. PSG's squad remained formidable heading into the campaign. The club retained its core attacking talent including Kvaratskhelia (€90m market value), Dembélé (€100m), and Barcola (€110m), while adding reinforcements like João Neves (€110m) and Désiré Doué (€90m) in midfield. Total squad market value stood around €1.2 billion (Transfermarkt). Manager Luis Enrique, who delivered the Champions League title, remained in place. However, sources noted potential fatigue concerns: PSG's extended 2024-25 season concluded in mid-July with a Club World Cup final loss to Chelsea, leaving minimal recovery time before the new campaign began (Flashscore). The nearest challengers faced significant obstacles. Lille, given 5.5% title probability by Opta, lost star striker Jonathan David (16 Ligue 1 goals in 2024-25) to Juventus and finished fifth the prior season. Monaco (4.2% probability) and Marseille (3.4%) were distant alternatives. Marseille, despite adding Mason Greenwood as a goal threat, faced consistency questions under manager Roberto De Zerbi. Lyon, who narrowly avoided Ligue 2 demotion via appeal, carried institutional instability into the season. Historical context reinforces PSG's dominance: only Monaco (2016-17) and Lille (2020-21) have won Ligue 1 since 2012-13. PSG won 11 of the last 13 titles. Betting markets offered "winner without PSG" odds at 9/4 for Monaco and 5/2 for Marseille, underscoring the two-tier competitive structure. **Most important facts:** (1) PSG held 76-79% title probability across independent models and bookmakers, with odds as low as 1/10; (2) PSG finished 19 points ahead of second place in 2024-25 and won four consecutive titles; (3) Nearest challengers Lille, Monaco, and Marseille each held single-digit title probabilities, with Lille weakened by David's departure.

iterations.content: I have enough signal. As of late November 2025, after 13-14 games, the race is tight: Marseille briefly went top after a 5-1 win at Nice (28 pts), and PSG lost to Monaco on Nov 29 (their second Ligue 1 defeat). So PSG is no longer running away with it. Let me check standings more carefully.

SYNTHESIZED SUMMARY (debiased, pre-cutoff sources only):
After the match, PSG remained in first place with 30 points from 14 games, while Monaco moved to sixth place with 23 points. • **November 29, 2025** — Yahoo Sports/AFP — PSG suffered a shock 1-0 defeat at Monaco but retained top spot in Ligue 1 with 30 points, one point ahead of Marseille (28 points). Lens was in third place, two points behind the leaders. • **November 29, 2025** — beIN SPORTS — Monaco's 1-0 victory over PSG ended their three-game losing streak and put PSG's Ligue 1 lead in jeopardy. The result meant PSG could lose their place at the top if Marseille won their match against Toulouse. • **November 29, 2025** — ESPN Soccer (Standings Table) — The Ligue 1 standings showed: PSG 1st (30 pts, 14 GP), Marseille 2nd (28 pts, 13 GP), Lens 3rd (28 pts, 13 GP), Rennes 4th (24 pts, 14 GP), Lille 5th (23 pts, 13 GP), and Monaco 6th (23 pts, 14 GP).

Only a "miracle" would prevent PSG from claiming another consecutive crown. • **[August 2025]** Khel Now — https://khelnow.com/football/world-football-ligue-1-2025-26-title-favourites-top-four-relegation-battle-more-202508 — The Opta supercomputer gives PSG a 78.7% chance of winning the title for a fifth straight season, while Marseille and Lyon each have 3.4% chances. Marseille have a 43% likelihood of finishing in the top four. • **[August 15, 2025]** FotMob — https://www.fotmob.com/el/news/9kkp22g27zu1de6lyraf3mws-ligue-1-2025-26-psgs-dominance-expected-continue-expect-tight-champions-league-fight — PSG are given 78.7% chance of lifting the trophy for a fifth consecutive season, while Marseille have 3.4% odds and are expected to have a 43% chance of finishing in the top four. • **[Pre-season 2025]** Racing Post — https://www.racingpost.com/sport/football-tips/european-football/ligue-1-predictions-title-odds-and-best-bets-aE0bN6h1wdM

args.reasoning:
Evidence inventory (specific, recent facts):
- Nov 29, 2025 (ESPN/Yahoo/AFP): PSG lost 1-0 at Monaco (Minamino goal), their 2nd Ligue 1 defeat of the season. Standings after MD14: PSG 30 pts (14 GP), Marseille 28 (13 GP), Lens 28 (13 GP), Rennes 24, Lille 23, Monaco 23. PSG retained top spot but only by 1-2 points with Marseille having a game in hand.
- Pre-season: Opta supercomputer gave PSG 78.7% to win 5th straight title; Marseille 3.4%; bookmakers had PSG at 1.10. PSG remains dominant squad-wise but title race is tighter than usual.
- Kalshi has PSG at 0.98, which seems aggressive given the actual standings show Marseille only 1 point back with a game in hand. Marseille (under De Zerbi, with Greenwood scoring) has won at Nice 5-1 recently and is genuinely challenging.

Implication: PSG is still the clear favorite — deepest squad, defending champs four straight, leads table — but with ~24 matches remaining and Marseille/Lens within 1-2 points, a 98% probability overstates certainty. A more reasonable PSG probability is ~88-92%. Marseille has the most realistic path as the closest challenger with a game in hand and strong recent form. Lens (also 28 pts) is overperforming and historically fades. Monaco beat PSG but sits 6th at 23 pts.

Upset check: No scandal/manager change at PSG. They lost 2 league games but remain top. Marseille's surge is a genuine STRONG signal — they're 1 pt back with game in hand. This justifies elevating Marseille above its preseason 3.4%.

Final allocation: PSG ~90%, Marseille ~6%, Lens ~1.5%, Monaco ~0.8%, others minimal.

args.reasoning:
The dominant factor for the 2025-26 season is the departure of Kylian Mbappé from PSG to Real Madrid, confirmed in early June 2024. This is the most significant roster change in Ligue 1 in over a decade and materially weakens the Parisian side, which has relied on him as its central attacking figure. His absence removes a guaranteed source of goals and attacking threat, potentially making the title race more competitive than it has been in years.

However, PSG's systemic advantages remain overwhelming. Evidence from early 2024 shows the club was already planning for a post-Mbappé era, with Napoli's striker Victor Osimhen identified as a primary target. This demonstrates PSG's intent and, crucially, its continued financial capacity to attract world-class talent to replace departing stars. The club's financial backing from Qatar Sports Investments allows it to maintain a squad with depth and quality that far exceeds any domestic rival.

While teams like Monaco, Lille, and Lyon will look to capitalize on a potentially vulnerable PSG, there is no strong evidence that they have made the transformative signings necessary to consistently challenge for the title over a 38-game season. PSG has won 10 of the last 12 Ligue 1 titles, establishing a powerful base rate of success. The loss of Mbappé is a significant headwind, but the club's financial might and proactive approach to recruitment make them strong favorites to secure another title.

iterations.content:
The five forecasters disagree significantly on PSG's probability:
- High-confidence narrative agents: 0.96-0.97 (treating Kalshi @ 0.98 as informative)
- Deep research agents: 0.66-0.71 (citing November 2025 PSG defeat at Monaco, only 2-point lead after MD14)

The key disagreement is FACTUAL/MAGNITUDE: how far into the season are we, and how dominant is PSG? DEEP_0 cites MD14 (Nov 29) with PSG only 2 points ahead. But the question is T-3d from resolution (end of season ~May 2026), and Kalshi pricing PSG at 0.98 strongly suggests near-clinched.

My search confirms: after MD27, PSG leads by 8 points over Monaco, 11 over Marseille. By later matchdays (60 pts after 26 matches in one source, holds dominant +36 GD). This strongly supports the narrative agents — PSG has rebuilt their dominant lead after the November wobble. The deep research agents anchored too heavily on November snapshots and missed the recovery.

Kalshi at 0.98 with T-3d remaining is highly informative. The narrative cluster (0.95-0.97) is correct.

args.reasoning: Key disagreement: PSG probability split between narrative agents (0.95-0.97) anchoring on Kalshi @ 0.98 and preseason Opta 78.7%, vs deep research agents (0.66-0.71) anchoring on PSG's Nov 29 loss at Monaco leaving them only 2 points clear after MD14. This is a FACTUAL/MAGNITUDE disagreement about current season state. My search resolved it: after MD27, PSG leads Monaco by 8 points and Marseille by 11, with best attack and defense. Later snapshots show PSG with 60+ pts and +36 GD — they recovered decisively from the November wobble. The deep agents over-anchored on a single stale data point. With T-3d to resolution and Kalshi @ 0.98, PSG is essentially clinched or one win away. Mathematically, with an 8-11 point lead and few matches remaining, the title is near-certain. I side with the narrative cluster around 0.96. Residual ~4% accounts for the (very small) tail of a not-yet-mathematically-clinched scenario plus market-implied uncertainty. Distributing the rest mostly to Monaco/Marseille/Lens (the actual challengers per current standings).
